response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack/web software developer with strong TypeScript, Node.js, and D-language backgrounds, but the role requires deep hardware-adjacent expertise — SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appears in the resume. Brief exposure to C/C++ tooling at National Instruments and low-latency DLang services is the closest match, but it falls far short of principal-level performance architecture experience for deep-learning workloads.
strengths: Broad, long-tenured software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded contexts
Low-latency systems experience handling millions of requests per second (DLang, async I/O, fibers)
Strong software craftsmanship practices: TDD, CI/CD, open-source authorship, teaching and workshops
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon, or hardware architecture experience whatsoever
No deep-learning training/inference optimization or GPU/accelerator workload analysis background
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, not performance architecture at the hardware-software boundary

key_factors.factor: Missing SoC/IP modeling and hardware co-design experience
No AI accelerator or DNN performance optimization background
General low-latency systems and C/C++ exposure
